trivia: When it flew in 2001 this was the longest airliner ever built, a 75-meter four-engined giant with Trent 500s. High fuel costs ended its career early, and active airframes are now genuinely scarce.
So long that a tail camera was fitted just to help pilots taxi without scrubbing the main gear on turns.
funFacts: At 75.36 m it held the world length record until the 747-8.
Has an underfloor galley and crew area below the passenger deck.
